beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, and check the day ahead from home.
beats.reasoning: Aiden starts the week energized and full. Early rise to enjoy the sunny morning and plan around the FIFA match and Light Rail closure.

beats.activity: Drive from home to the Fremont studio.
beats.reasoning: Commute from West Seattle to Fremont; scenic Monday drive to settle into work mode before the day's social events.

beats.activity: Set up studio and prep for the day; review client consultations and ink supplies.
beats.reasoning: Morning prep work before the first appointment; Aiden is meticulous and takes time to ensure the studio is perfect.

beats.activity: Walk to a nearby café for a quick breakfast and to catch the start of the USA vs Wales match.
beats.location_name: Molly Moon's Homemade Ice Cream (Fremont, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Fremont is walkable and social; Aiden takes a break to grab a bite and tune into the 11am FIFA match rather than stay isolated at the studio.

beats.activity: Watch the USA vs Wales match at a local bar with friends.
beats.reasoning: The match airs at 11am; Aiden values time with close friends over big crowds, so chooses a smaller neighborhood bar in Fremont rather than crowded downtown.
Short walk back to the studio to resume afternoon client appointments; Fremont is compact and Aiden enjoys the walk.

beats.activity: Afternoon tattoo client sessions and studio work.
beats.reasoning: Steady afternoon workload; Aiden is a perfectionist and dedicates focused time to client consultations and ink work.

beats.activity: Drive from Fremont studio toward home, taking the scenic route along the water.
beats.reasoning: Aiden always takes the scenic route; evening drive home via waterfront roads to unwind after a full day of work and social time.

beats.activity: Trail run along nearby West Seattle trails to enjoy the sunny evening.
beats.reasoning: High of 74°F and sunny; Aiden loves trail running and takes advantage of the perfect early-June weather for an evening workout before winding down.

beats.activity: Return home, shower, and prepare a simple dinner.
beats.reasoning: Back at home after exercise; Aiden showers and cooks a light meal to refuel after the trail run.

beats.activity: Relax with thrifted finds and listen to music; browse online thrift listings.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down evening at home; Aiden enjoys thrifting as a hobby and spends time browsing and organizing recent finds before bed.
Full night of sleep after an active, social Monday; 8 hours of rest to prepare for the week ahead.

diary: Woke up to perfect weather and that restless Monday energy. The studio felt good this morning—got everything prepped, inks organized, the space humming. Around ten I ditched for The Walrus to catch the USA match with some folks. Nothing like watching soccer with a bar full of people who actually care, you know? The energy was electric, even if the game was scrappy. Headed back and got into a solid afternoon of client work—did a beautiful piece on this woman's shoulder, all geometric lines and subtle color work. Felt good to be in the zone. After work I took the long way home along the water, windows down, just breathing. Then hit Discovery Park for a run as the sun was still high. The trails were perfect, legs felt strong. Back home now, showered off the day, got some thrifted vinyl spinning and a bowl of pasta. Just scrolling through some estate sale listings online. There's something about Mondays like this—the sun, the work, the movement—that makes everything feel possible.
thought_process: I was genuinely energized this morning, maybe because of the weather or just the rhythm of getting back to work after the weekend. The World Cup match felt like a gift—I almost never take breaks mid-morning, but the bars opening early and the buzz around it was contagious. I figured why not? It reminded me I'm not just grinding at the studio; I'm part of a community. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I drive, but I was aware of it affecting others around the city. The afternoon client sessions felt focused and rewarding, which put me in a great headspace. By evening I needed to move—the sunshine had wound me up in a good way, so the run was necessary, not optional. Now I'm in that sweet spot between tired and content, and browsing thrift listings is my version of meditation. Today felt balanced.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
